role.description: What the feature is for. Omitted = combat. COMPOSITION RULE: every subclass has exactly 3 features — 2 combat + 1 roleplay (for dialogue, exploration, improvised attempts). Acrobat disciplines are the sanctioned 4-feature exception (their L5 reaction feature).

sheetSummary.description: REQUIRED EVENTUALLY on every feature (backfill before character creation ships). The condensed text shown on a player's character sheet: ONLY what it does — effect, damage + type, conditions applied, cost/uses. Cut all flavor and all 'why/how the class performs it' prose. Features with `options` keep their full options table on the sheet regardless (Wild Card and the like are pertinent info).

meta.description: At-a-glance combat fields shown as labelled chips below the feature (D&D Beyond style). All optional.

options.description: For a feature that offers a menu of choices or a random table. The renderer shows these as a table; `description` holds the intro text. Author any multi-option feature this way rather than cramming the options into prose.

roll.description: Optional die result for a random table. Omit for a pick-one menu.

effect.description: What it does. May contain [[XdY]] scaling-die tokens.
